# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    postgresql \
    postgresql-contrib \
    redis-server \
    supervisor \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN mkdir -p /var/log/supervisor /run/postgresql

RUN sed -i "s/^#\?listen_addresses.*/listen_addresses = '*'/" /etc/postgresql/*/main/postgresql.conf && \
    sed -i "s/^host\s\+all\s\+all\s\+127.0.0.1\/32\s\+scram-sha-256/host all all 0.0.0.0\/0 md5/" /etc/postgresql/*/main/pg_hba.conf && \
    echo "host all all 0.0.0.0/0 md5" >> /etc/postgresql/*/main/pg_hba.conf

RUN pip3 install flask gunicorn

RUN bash -lc 'cat > /app.py <<EOF
from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def hello():
    return "API server running"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=8080)
EOF'

RUN bash -lc 'cat > /etc/nginx/sites-available/default <<EOF
server {
    listen 80 default_server;
    listen [::]:80 default_server;
    location / {
        return 200 "Web server running\n";
        add_header Content-Type text/plain;
    }
}
EOF'

RUN bash -lc 'cat > /etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf <<EOF
[supervisord]
nodaemon=true

[program:nginx]
command=/usr/sbin/nginx -g "daemon off;"
autorestart=true
priority=10

[program:api]
command=/usr/local/bin/gunicorn --bind 0.0.0.0:8080 app:app
directory=/
autorestart=true
priority=20

[program:postgresql]
command=/usr/bin/bash -lc "service postgresql start && tail -f /dev/null"
autorestart=true
priority=30

[program:redis]
command=/usr/bin/redis-server --bind 0.0.0.0 --protected-mode no
autorestart=true
priority=40
EOF'

EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

CMD ["/usr/bin/supervisord", "-c", "/etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf"]
